title: Someone designed this
text:
Someone designed this

Why bad UX is often a business decision, not a design failure.

There’s a screen most people have seen at least once.

You’re trying to cancel a subscription. You’ve made up your mind. You click the button that looks like it starts the process.

And then something strange happens.

The page that loads isn’t a cancellation form. It’s a full-screen gallery of everything you’re about to lose. Videos. Deals. Music. Free delivery. Presented in bright tiles, warm colours, friendly icons. A highlighted countdown showing how many days you have left.

And somewhere at the bottom, small, grey, easy to miss, the option to continue cancelling.

You almost didn’t see it.

That was the point.

Someone designed that screen. Someone decided where to put the button. Someone chose that colour. Someone wrote that copy. Someone reviewed the design, ran the numbers, and approved it.

And the number they were looking at wasn’t your satisfaction score.

The half of UX nobody puts in a portfolio

Every portfolio you’ve ever read has some version of this:

“I improved onboarding and increased conversion by 34%.” “I redesigned checkout and reduced drop-off by 22%.”

Look at what every single one of those metrics measures.

Getting users in.

The entire credibility of the UX industry was built on that half of the journey. Designers got seats at tables, budgets, job titles, because we could prove that better experience meant more revenue. It was true. It still is.

But only in one direction.

The moment a user tries to leave, really tries, with intent, the relationship between good design and good business quietly breaks down. And almost nobody in the industry talks about what happens next.

Early in my career, I worked on a financial product redesign. Depositing money was frictionless, one click, multiple payment methods, no verification needed. Withdrawing was a completely different experience. Verification only kicked in when money was going out. Minimum balance rules meant you could never take everything you earned. Conversion rates weren’t visible until the very end.

The client knew. It wasn’t illegal. But the design was built to serve one direction.

I was a junior designer. The decisions came from above. I designed what I was asked to design. I didn’t speak up, not because I didn’t feel something was off, but because I didn’t yet know how to put it into words.

I’m a product designer. I sit in those reviews. I’ve seen that moment from the inside, when a metric goes up, and something quietly feels wrong. This is about what’s on the other side of that feeling.

The name they gave it says everything

Between 2016 and 2023, Amazon’s internal name for their Prime cancellation process was “The Iliad Flow.”

Named after Homer’s epic poem about the decade-long Trojan War.

They named their own cancellation system after one of history’s longest and most gruelling journeys. Not ironically. As a design brief.

  • Signing up for Prime: 2 clicks
  • Cancelling Prime: 4 pages. 6 clicks. 15 options.

And before you even reached those 4 pages, you had to find the entrance. That meant opening the “Account & Lists” dropdown, navigating to the third column of links, and selecting the eleventh option in that column.

The FTC calls this Obstruction, also known as the Roach Motel technique, a term first documented by UX researcher Harry Brignull in 2010.

Designed so you never check out. Amazon had known since at least 2018 that users couldn’t find this link. They left it there anyway.

Once you found the entrance, here’s what waited inside. These are screenshots submitted as evidence in federal court, not mockups, not recreations:

Dark pattern #1: Misdirection

You clicked cancel. This is what loaded. Not a form, a showcase. Every Prime benefit in bright, engaging tiles. The actual path to continue cancelling is the small text at the bottom. The FTC’s term: misdirection. Design that redirects your attention away from your goal.

Dark pattern #2: Confirm-shaming

Screen two. The copy reads: “you will no longer be eligible for your unclaimed Prime exclusive offers.” Confirm-shaming. Language engineered to make cancelling feel like a mistake you’re making. The cancel option is still not the most prominent thing on screen.

Dark pattern #3: Interface interference

Screen three. A bright yellow button pushes toward Prime benefits. Warning icons appear near the cancel option to trigger anxiety. Visual hierarchy used deliberately against the user’s intent. The FTC calls this interface interference.

This wasn’t a rogue designer. This was a documented, deliberate system reviewed at executive level.

When someone at Amazon proposed simplifying cancellation, the proposal was rejected. The reason given, in writing: “Easier exits hurt subscription revenue.”

An internal memo called it “a bit of a shady world.” Another called unwanted subscriptions “an unspoken cancer.”

The people who wrote those words kept working on the product. For seven years.

And then there’s this. The detail that should make you stop completely:

Whenever Amazon made cancellation easier, Prime sign-ups dropped. So they made it hard again.

Read that once more.

Easier to leave meant fewer people joining. The friction wasn’t a side effect. It was load-bearing. Remove it and the whole business shifts.

That is the exact moment where good UX and profitable UX are not the same thing. Someone knew. Someone chose.

But this isn’t only an executive failure. Every product cycle starts with targets, improve retention by 10%, reduce churn, move the NPS. Designers working toward those goals don’t consciously decide to make cancellation harder. But the small decisions compound in one direction. The primary button goes to “Keep membership.” Cancel becomes a ghost button, or a text link. A retention offer screen appears before the exit. Each decision follows standard design hierarchy. Each one feels justified. The metric goes up. Nobody connects the dots.

That’s what makes this structural. It doesn’t require bad intent. It just requires a goal that only measures one half of the journey.

It wasn’t just Amazon

In 2024, regulators from 26 countries audited 642 subscription products globally. What they found:

  • 76% had at least one dark pattern built in
  • 67% had more than one
  • 81% made auto-renewal impossible to turn off during sign-up
  • 70% gave users no information on how to cancel

This wasn’t a study of bad companies. It was a cross-section of the industry.

And the companies caught in enforcement actions paid for it:

Amazon paid $2.5 billion for a 4-page cancellation maze that trapped 35 million people. $1.5 billion goes directly back to affected consumers. FTC v. Amazon →

Epic Games paid $245 million for button layouts that charged Fortnite players for items they were just trying to preview. Kids racked up hundreds of dollars. When parents disputed the charges, Epic locked their accounts. FTC v. Epic Games →

Google / YouTube paid $170 million for collecting children’s data without parental consent. FTC v. Google / YouTube →

HelloFresh paid $7.5 million for a cancellation flow deliberately designed to stop people from leaving. FTC v. HelloFresh →

These aren’t companies that made mistakes. They’re companies with design systems, user research, and A/B testing infrastructure who looked at the data and kept going anyway.

The assumption nobody questioned

The UX industry has one founding belief: good experience is good business.

It’s what got designers into strategy meetings. And it’s true, for the part of the journey we were allowed to see. Acquisition, onboarding, conversion, better UX, better numbers, everyone wins.

But in 2024, the FTC passed a rule that said the quiet part out loud. The Click-to-Cancel rule required companies to make cancellation as simple as sign-up. Regulators had seen enough evidence that companies were deliberately making it harder. The rule was later vacated on a procedural technicality, not because the principle was wrong, but because the FTC skipped a required economic analysis step.

The dark patterns are still legal. For now.

“Too often, businesses make people jump through endless hoops just to cancel a subscription. Nobody should be stuck paying for a service they no longer want.” — FTC Chair Lina M. Khan, October 2024

The incentive is structural. It doesn’t require bad people. It just requires a business model where confusion is more profitable than honesty.

That’s the conversation most design reviews never have.

The question worth asking before you ship

“Does this design make it as easy to leave as it is to join?”
“If not, is that a product decision or a design decision? And who owns it?”

These aren’t uncomfortable questions. They’re the ones that separate design that serves users from design that just serves revenue.

But they’re hard to ask out loud. In a room full of stakeholders focused on revenue, “this feels wrong for users” gets dismissed before you finish the sentence.

The designers who get heard reframe it:

  • Not as an ethics problem alone, but as a business risk
  • Not just as user empathy, but as brand trust
  • Not as a feeling, but as a liability with a price tag

A user who feels trapped doesn’t just cancel quietly. They tell people. That cost doesn’t show up this quarter. It shows up later, when nobody can explain why trust dropped.

The regulatory pressure is real and it’s coming from every direction. The US FTC is actively fining companies. The EU’s Digital Services Act explicitly prohibits manipulative design. India’s Central Consumer Protection Authority introduced specific dark pattern guidelines in 2023, covering 13 identified patterns, with enforcement already happening.

Nobody cared about accessibility until ADA compliance became impossible to ignore. Then it was in every design system, every sprint, every audit checklist, not because designers became more ethical, but because ignoring it had a real cost.

Dark patterns are heading the same way. The laws are here. The fines are happening.

Designers who raise it now, framing it as risk rather than just conscience, are the ones who get heard.

Amazon had years of internal evidence that their cancellation flow was hurting real people. They had designers, researchers, product managers, and executives reviewing it regularly. Nobody asked those questions out loud. At least not loud enough.

They paid $2.5 billion to answer them in court instead.

You can ask them in your next sprint review. For free.

Screenshots: FTC v. Amazon, Case 2:23-cv-00932, public federal court document, filed June 21, 2023, U.S. District Court for the Western District of Washington.

Sources, all verified official government documents

FTC v. Amazon main case page · FTC $2.5B settlement · FTC complaint PDF with screenshots pages 44 to 52 · FTC ICPEN Sweep Report 2024 · FTC Click-to-Cancel Rule · FTC v. Epic Games · FTC v. Google YouTube · FTC v. HelloFresh

title: All The Early Fourth of July Fashion Sales You Can Shop Already
text:
All The Early Fourth of July Fashion Sales You Can Shop Already

If you didn’t get to cart up everything you needed from Prime Day, just know that Fourth of July fashion sales are already firing up — like all the barbecues and fireworks we’re excited to enjoy. But unlike a fleeting firecracker, these stellar markdowns are here well before the holiday weekend.

We’ve searched for the best early Fourth of July sales across fashion, including clothing, bag, and shoe deals that the internet has to offer — and found a host from reader-favorite retailers like Lululemon, Anthropologie, DSW, and Zara.

Whether you’re looking for summer staples like matching sets and trendy swimsuits or travel sneakers, there are plenty of brands treating us to amazing markdowns — with some offering discounts as high as 70% off.

Ahead, peruse 30+ early Fourth of July sales worth shopping while the summer fashion deals are hot.

DashDividers_1_500x100

Abercrombie & Fitch Sale

During the Summer Sale, take between 20% and 40% off almost everything through July 1.

American Eagle Sale

During the Beach to BBQ Sale, take 30% to 50% off all American Eagle and Aerie styles, including new arrivals through July 6.

Ann Taylor Sale

Take 40% off your full-price purchase, including new arrivals through July 2.

Anthropologie Sale

During the Summer Sale, take an extra 50% off sale and final sale styles through July 5.

Aritzia Sale

During the Summer Sale, take up to 50% off everything through July 5.

Bloomingdale’s Sale

During the Designer Sale, take between 30% and 60% off select designer styles for a limited time.

Calpak Sale

During the July 4th Sale, take up to 60% off sitewide, including luggage, packing cubes, backpacks, and travel accessories through July 9. Plus, get a free bag charm on qualifying purchases with code SUMMER for a limited time.

Cinq à Sept Sale

During the Summer Sale, take up to 65% off with an extra 30% off select styles (automatically applied at checkout) through July 6.

Damson Madder Sale

During the Summer Sale, take up to 40% off select styles for a limited time.

DeMellier Sale

During the Summer Sale, shop exclusive discounts on handbags for a limited time.

DSW Sale

During the Semi-Annual Sale, take 40% off clearance in stores, or an additional 10% off clearance online with code FANTASTIC through July 5.

Gap Sale

During the Summer Sale, take 50% off shorts, tees, swim, and more through July 2.

Garage Sale

During the Summer Sale, take 50% off select styles through July 5.

Good American Sale

During the Summer Sale, take 25% off sitewide with code SUMMER, with some exclusions, through July 5.

Hill House Home Sale

During the Semi Annual Sale, take an extra 20% off select styles for a limited time.

J.Crew Sale

During the Summer Sale, take an extra 50% off sale styles with code SUMMER, plus 50% off select summer styles for a limited time.

Lands’ End Sale

During the Summer End of Season Sale, take up to 70% off apparel, swim, and more, including petite and plus size styles for a limited time.

Lululemon Sale

During the Summer Sale, shop deals on select styles, including apparel, accessories, and shoes for a limited time.

Macy’s Sale

Shop the Underwear & Lingerie Sale through July 5, plus the Great Sandal Sale through July 6.

Margaux Sale

During the Annual Archive Sale, take up to 50% off retiring silhouettes, past-season colors, and previous collaborations through July 7.

Net-a-Porter Sale

Take up to 60% off select luxury fashion for a limited time.

Nordstrom Rack Sale

title: Your July Horoscope Is Here — & It’ll Be A Month Like No Other
text:
Your July Horoscope Is Here — & It’ll Be A Month Like No Other

Welcome to July 2026, a month like no other. The biggest story is Jupiter, the planet of luck, abundance, and expansion, spending its first full month in Leo in over a decade. Jupiter magnifies everything it touches, and because Leo is ruled by the sun, every zodiac sign is being encouraged to shine more boldly and take up the space they were born to occupy. Even the quietest souls may feel a growing desire to create, lead, perform, launch, or finally let themselves be seen. Just remember that confidence and external validation are not the same thing. One is rooted in self-trust. The other disappears as quickly as the applause.

Fortunately, the cosmos gives us a built-in reality check. Mercury continues its retrograde through Cancer until July 23rd, encouraging us to slow down, reflect, and reconnect with what genuinely matters before rushing toward the spotlight. Neptune also stations retrograde in Aries on July 7th, while Venus leaves Leo for Virgo on July 9th, bringing a more discerning and grounded energy to the month. Rather than asking, “How do I become more successful?” these transits ask, “Why do I want this in the first place?” If your motivations are rooted in purpose instead of performance, Jupiter in Leo can work wonders.

The Cancer New Moon on July 14th arrives in the heart of Mercury retrograde, making it a beautiful time to return to a dream, project, or calling you quietly set aside earlier this year. Sometimes the most life-changing fresh start isn’t something brand new. It’s finally giving your full attention to something your soul has been patiently waiting for you to finish. If there were one meaningful goal you would love to complete before the end of 2026, this is the lunation to plant that intention and begin nurturing it with care.

The second half of the month feels like a dramatic turning point. The sun enters Leo on July 22nd, Mercury stations direct one day later, and momentum begins building quickly. Then, on July 26th, Saturn stations retrograde in Aries while the lunar nodes shift into the Aquarius-Leo axis, beginning a brand-new eighteen-month chapter focused on balancing personal leadership with collective responsibility. The month closes with a powerful Aquarius Full Moon on July 29th, reminding us that our greatest gifts aren’t meant to be hidden or hoarded. The future belongs to those who know how to celebrate their individuality while remembering that none of us rises alone.

Read your horoscopes for your Sun and Rising signs for the most in-depth forecast.

DashDividers_1_500x100

Aries Sun & Rising:

Aries, July feels like the month where the spotlight finally finds you, but first it asks one important question: do you actually believe you deserve to be seen? Jupiter is spending its first full month in Leo, activating your creativity, romance, inner child, and visibility sector for the first time in over a decade. This is one of the luckiest transits you’ll experience this decade, but Cancer Season and Mercury retrograde in your home and family sector remind you that real confidence is built from the inside out. If old insecurities, family dynamics, or scarcity mindsets begin resurfacing, don’t view them as setbacks. They’re simply revealing what still wants your attention before your next chapter takes off. The stronger your roots become this month, the brighter you’ll shine over the coming year.

Neptune stations retrograde on July 7th, followed by Saturn retrograde in your sign on the 26th, making July a deeply reflective month beneath all of Jupiter’s excitement. You may find yourself reconsidering goals you once chased automatically or realizing that certain versions of success no longer resonate. This transit’s about making sure you’re climbing the right mountain. Trust the pause. The more intentional your decisions become now, the less energy you’ll waste proving yourself to people whose opinions no longer define your worth.

The final week of July shifts everything. Mercury stations direct, Leo season officially begins, and the lunar nodes move into Aquarius and Leo, activating a brand-new eighteen-month chapter around community, leadership, creativity, and purpose. The Aquarius Full Moon on July 29th may bring an exciting collaboration, meaningful friendship, or opportunity that reminds you your dreams were never meant to be built alone. Jupiter may be putting your name in lights, but this next season of your life also teaches you the power of finding the right people to stand beside you once the curtain rises.

Taurus Sun & Rising:

Taurus, if the past eight years felt like life constantly asking you to reinvent yourself, July is when you finally get to ask, “Now what do I want?” Jupiter is spending its first full month in Leo, expanding your home, family, and emotional foundation sector for the first time in over a decade. Some Taureans may be thinking about moving, renovating, growing their family, or simply creating a life that feels more peaceful behind the scenes. At the same time, Mercury retrograde and Cancer Season activate your communication sector, making this an important month to revisit conversations, rewrite old narratives, and become more intentional with the way you speak to yourself and the people you love. Your words are laying the foundation for your next chapter just as much as your actions.

Neptune stations retrograde on July 7th, followed by Saturn retrograde on the 26th, both in your spirituality and healing sector. This month’s biggest breakthroughs may happen quietly. You might realize you’ve outgrown a fear you’ve carried for years, release a friendship dynamic that no longer feels reciprocal, or finally stop carrying responsibilities that were never yours to begin with. Give yourself permission to spend more time alone than usual if your intuition asks for it. Solitude isn’t isolation this month—it’s where clarity lives.

The final week of July feels like the beginning of a completely different story. Mercury stations direct, Leo Season picks up speed, and the lunar nodes move into Aquarius and Leo, activating your career and home axis for the next eighteen months. The Aquarius Full Moon on July 29th could bring a turning point around your purpose, reputation, or long-term ambitions. You may find yourself stepping into a leadership role while simultaneously redefining what success actually looks like. This next chapter is asking you to balance building a meaningful career with building a meaningful life.

Gemini Sun & Rising:

Gemini, this is the first July in over a decade where it feels like the universe is speaking your language. Jupiter’s spending its first full month in Leo, activating your communication, creativity, learning, and connection sector. Your ideas have range right now, and the right conversation could completely change the direction of your year. But with Mercury, your ruling planet, retrograde in Cancer and your money sector for most of the month, don’t rush to monetize every idea or say yes to every opportunity. July asks you to value your voice before asking the world to. Revisit your rates, your budget, your sense of self-worth, and the ways you’ve been undervaluing your gifts. The more secure you feel within yourself, the easier it becomes to communicate from a place of confidence instead of proving.

Neptune stations retrograde on July 7th, followed by Saturn retrograde on the 26th, both in your friendship, community, and future vision sector. This is where you’ll begin separating genuine alignment from convenience. Some friendships may naturally drift, others will deepen, and you may become much clearer about which communities genuinely inspire you to grow. Pay attention to the people who make you feel more like yourself rather than those who expect you to perform a version of yourself you’ve already outgrown.

The final week of July feels like a green light. Mercury stations direct, Leo season gains momentum, and the lunar nodes shift into Aquarius and Leo, activating your expansion and communication axis for the next eighteen months. The Aquarius Full Moon on July 29th could bring exciting news about travel, publishing, education, entrepreneurship, or a dream you’ve quietly been preparing for. The world is getting bigger, and so is your perspective. Stay curious, stay adaptable, and don’t be surprised if one conversation before the month is over opens a door you hadn’t even considered knocking on.

Cancer Sun & Rising:

Cancer, this is one of those months where what happens quietly ends up changing your life the loudest. Jupiter’s spending its first full month in Leo, expanding your money, confidence, and self-worth sector, reminding you that abundance begins with believing you deserve it. At the same time, your solar season continues and Mercury retrogrades in your sign until July 23rd, asking you to slow down before making major decisions. July isn’t about becoming a completely different person overnight. It’s about rediscovering who you’ve always been underneath everyone else’s expectations. The more honest you are with yourself this month, the easier it becomes to recognize which opportunities genuinely align with your values and which ones are simply distractions dressed up as success.

Neptune stations retrograde on July 7th, followed by Saturn retrograde on the 26th, both activating your career and purpose sector. You may find yourself questioning ambitions that once felt non-negotiable or redefining what success actually means at this stage of your life. If a professional path no longer excites you, don’t panic. These retrogrades are asking you to build a career that’s sustainable enough to support the life you actually want, not just the one you thought you were supposed to have.

The final week of July opens an entirely new chapter. Mercury stations direct, Leo season gains momentum, and the lunar nodes shift into Aquarius and Leo, activating your intimacy, shared resources, and self-worth axis for the next eighteen months. The Aquarius Full Moon on July 29th could bring an important financial breakthrough, a vulnerable conversation, or a realization about the kind of support you’re finally ready to receive. One of the biggest lessons of this next chapter is learning that independence and interdependence can coexist. You don’t have to carry everything by yourself to prove you’re strong.

Leo Sun & Rising:

Leo, welcome to one of the biggest chapters of your decade. Jupiter’s spending its first full month in your sign for the first time in over twelve years, and the universe is inviting you to take up space without apologizing for it. More visibility, more opportunities, more confidence, and more momentum are all available, but Mercury retrograde in Cancer and Cancer Season remind you that not every win needs to be announced the moment it happens. Some of your greatest breakthroughs this month will happen behind the scenes as you reconnect with your intuition, protect your peace, and become clear about the version of yourself you’re actually ready to introduce to the world. Fame means very little if you don’t recognize yourself in the person receiving it.

Neptune stations retrograde on July 7th, followed by Saturn retrograde on the 26th, both activating your expansion, travel, publishing, and belief sector. Over the next few months, you may rethink where you’re headed, what success means to you, or which dreams still feel worth pursuing. If a vision you’ve held for years no longer excites you, that’s valuable information, not failure. Give yourself permission to outgrow old ambitions. The strongest leaders aren’t the ones who stubbornly stick to the plan — they’re the ones who know when a bigger vision is calling.

The final week of July feels like someone opening the curtains after a long rehearsal. The sun enters your sign, Mercury stations direct, and the lunar nodes shift into Aquarius and Leo, beginning an eighteen-month journey across your relationship axis. The Aquarius Full Moon on July 29th may bring a defining moment in love, friendship, or business, reminding you that your glow-up isn’t meant to happen in isolation. Jupiter is expanding your sense of self, but the North Node in Aquarius will keep asking an equally important question: Who are you becoming when you’re in the right company?

Virgo Sun & Rising:

Virgo, July reminds you that your biggest opportunities often arrive through people, not just plans. Jupiter’s spending its first full month in Leo, activating your spirituality, healing, and subconscious sector, making this a quieter form of abundance than you may have expected. Rather than external rewards arriving all at once, you’re clearing emotional and mental space for the next chapter that’s preparing to unfold. Cancer Season and Mercury retrograde in your friendship and community sector encourage you to reconnect with people from your past, revisit long-term goals, and become more intentional about who you share your energy with. Some friendships may naturally deepen, while others quietly run their course. Trust what feels reciprocal.

Neptune stations retrograde on July 7th, followed by Saturn retrograde on the 26th, both activating your intimacy, shared resources, and transformation sector. This month’s inner work revolves around trust. You may rethink financial partnerships, emotional commitments, or the ways you’ve been giving too much without allowing yourself to receive in return. These retrogrades ask you to stop confusing self-suﬃciency with strength. There’s wisdom in discernment, but there’s also wisdom in letting yourself be supported by the people who’ve earned your trust.

The final week of July shifts the energy considerably. Mercury stations direct, Leo Season picks up speed, and the lunar nodes move into Aquarius and Leo, activating your wellness and healing axis for the next eighteen months. The Aquarius Full Moon on July 29th may inspire you to make meaningful changes to your routines, work-life balance, or relationship with your body. The future you’re building depends on the daily habits that quietly sustain them, one intentional choice at a time.

Libra Sun & Rising:

Libra, July’s the month you stop waiting for permission to pursue the life you’ve been imagining. Jupiter is spending its first full month in Leo, activating your friendship, community, and future vision sector, making this one of the best times in over a decade to expand your network, collaborate, and dream bigger than you have in years. The right introduction, mentor, or community could change the trajectory of your life. At the same time, Cancer Season and Mercury retrograde activate your career sector, reminding you that your next professional chapter deserves careful reflection before bold action. Revisit your goals, refine your strategy, and ask yourself whether the success you’re chasing is still the success you actually want.

Neptune stations retrograde on July 7th, followed by Saturn retrograde on the 26th, both activating your relationship sector. These retrogrades may quietly reshape the way you approach love, friendship, and business partnerships over the coming months. Some Libras will become more aware of where they’ve been compromising too much to keep the peace. Others may realize they’re ready to commit more deeply to relationships that consistently feel safe, reciprocal, and emotionally mature. Healthy partnership is about creating enough trust to move through conflict together.

The final week of July feels like a turning point. Mercury stations direct, Leo Season gains momentum, and the lunar nodes shift into Aquarius and Leo, activating your creativity and community axis for the next eighteen months. The Aquarius Full Moon on July 29th may inspire you to launch a passion project, reconnect with your inner child, or finally share a talent you’ve been keeping to yourself. This next chapter asks you to stop treating your gifts like hobbies if they’re secretly one of the reasons you’re here. The people you’re meant to impact are much easier to find once you let yourself be fully seen.

Scorpio Sun & Rising:

Scorpio, July asks you to stop playing smaller than the life you’re capable of living. Jupiter’s spending its first full month in Leo, activating your career, reputation, and legacy sector for the first time in over a decade. Doors can open professionally that once felt permanently closed, and people are paying closer attention to your work, leadership, and vision. At the same time, Cancer Season and Mercury retrograde activate your expansion sector, reminding you that the biggest opportunities come from widening your perspective. So use this transit to revisit the business idea, plan the trip, or return to the course or certification you put on hold. Sometimes your next level begins by finishing what your past self started.

Neptune stations retrograde on July 7th, followed by Saturn retrograde on the 26th, both activating your wellness, work, and routines sector. This month asks you to take an honest look at how your daily habits either support or sabotage the future you’re trying to build. You don’t need a completely different life; you may simply need a different rhythm. If your schedule has become unsustainable, your body has been asking for more rest, or you’ve been pouring energy into responsibilities that no longer align with your purpose, these retrogrades will make it difficult to ignore. Small, consistent adjustments now will have a much greater impact than dramatic overnight changes.

The final week of July marks the beginning of a powerful eighteen-month chapter. Mercury stations direct, Leo Season gains momentum, and the lunar nodes shift into Aquarius and Leo, activating your home-career axis. The Aquarius Full Moon on July 29th may bring clarity around where you want to live, who you want to build a life with, or what success actually looks like beyond external achievement. Jupiter’s encouraging you to dream bigger professionally, while the North Node in Aquarius reminds you that the strongest legacy is one that’s built on a foundation that feels just as fulfilling in private as it does in public.

Sagittarius Sun & Rising:

Sag, July reminds you that the life you’re dreaming about isn’t somewhere else — it’s waiting on the other side of one bold decision. Jupiter, your ruling planet, is spending its first full month in Leo, activating your expansion, travel, entrepreneurship, and higher learning sector for the first time in over a decade. This is one of the luckiest transits you’ll experience this decade, making it an incredible month to think bigger, say yes to adventure, share your expertise, or finally pursue the opportunity that’s been quietly calling your name. At the same time, Cancer Season and Mercury retrograde activate your intimacy and transformation sector, reminding you that growth isn’t only about where you’re going… it’s also about what you’re willing to release. Before you can fully step into your next adventure, you may need to let go of an old fear, financial burden, or version of yourself that no longer fits.

Neptune stations retrograde on July 7th, followed by Saturn retrograde on the 26th, both activating your creativity, romance, and inner child sector. This month asks you to reconnect with what genuinely lights you up instead of what simply keeps you busy. You may rediscover a creative passion, rethink a romantic dynamic, or realize you’ve been taking your gifts more seriously than your joy. The strongest ideas you’ll have this month will arrive when you give yourself permission to play, experiment, and create without needing every outcome mapped out in advance.

The final week of July opens an exciting new eighteen-month chapter. Mercury stations direct, Leo Season picks up speed, and the lunar nodes shift into Aquarius and Leo, activating your communication and expansion axis. The Aquarius Full Moon on July 29th could bring exciting news, an unexpected collaboration, or the confidence to finally share a message you’ve been holding back. Your words carry more influence than you realize. Jupiter’s expanding your worldview, while the North Node in Aquarius reminds you that the knowledge you’re gathering has the power to transform both your life and someone else’s.

Capricorn Sun & Rising:

Capricorn, July reminds you that some of the biggest transformations in your life happen quietly, long before anyone else notices them. Jupiter’s spending its first full month in Leo, activating your intimacy, merging, and transformation sector for the first time in over a decade. This can be an incredibly abundant transit financially and emotionally, especially if you’re willing to collaborate, invest wisely, or allow yourself to receive more support than you’re used to. At the same time, Cancer Season and Mercury retrograde activate your relationship sector, asking you to revisit important conversations with romantic partners, close friends, family members, or business collaborators. Don’t rush to fix everything immediately. Sometimes listening more deeply reveals solutions you couldn’t have arrived at through logic alone.

Neptune stations retrograde on July 7th, followed by Saturn retrograde on the 26th, both activating your home, family, and emotional foundation sector. This month’s work is deeply personal. You may find yourself reflecting on childhood patterns, family responsibilities, living situations, or the emotional habits that shaped the way you show up in the world today. Use this retrograde to decide which parts of your past deserve to come with you into your future. The stronger your foundation becomes, the easier it’ll be to build everything else you’re working toward.

The final week of July marks the beginning of an entirely new eighteen-month cycle. Mercury stations direct, Leo Season gains momentum, and the lunar nodes shift into Aquarius and Leo, activating your money and shared resources axis. The Aquarius Full Moon on July 29th may bring an important financial realization, a new income opportunity, or a shift in the way you define security and abundance. Over the next year and a half, you’ll discover that wealth is about creating enough stability that you’re free to make decisions from alignment instead of fear.

Aquarius Sun & Rising:

Aquarius, July feels like the beginning of a new era in the way you love, collaborate, and let yourself be supported. Jupiter’s spending its first full month in Leo, activating your relationship sector for the first time in over a decade. This is one of the luckiest periods in years for attracting meaningful romantic, platonic, and professional partnerships. Existing relationships can deepen, new ones can arrive unexpectedly, and the right collaboration could completely change your trajectory. At the same time, Cancer Season and Mercury retrograde activate your wellness and routines sector, reminding you that the healthiest relationships begin with the relationship you have with yourself. As exciting as new connections may be, don’t neglect your sleep, your body, your boundaries, or the habits that keep you grounded. The strongest partnerships are built by two people who are each taking responsibility for their own well-being.

Neptune stations retrograde on July 7th, followed by Saturn retrograde on the 26th, both activating your communication, learning, and mindset sector. Over the next few months, you’ll become much more aware of the stories you tell yourself every day. Some beliefs you’ve carried for years may no longer fit the person you’re becoming. You’ll also notice which conversations leave you feeling inspired and which ones simply drain your energy. This is a beautiful time to write, teach, study, journal, or finally give a voice to ideas you’ve been sitting on. Your perspective is evolving, and your words are beginning to reflect that evolution.

The final week of July marks one of the biggest turning points of your year. Mercury stations direct, Leo Season gains momentum, and the lunar nodes shift into Aquarius and Leo, beginning a brand-new eighteen-month cycle across your identity and relationship axis. The Aquarius Full Moon on July 29th feels especially personal, bringing clarity about who you are, who you’re becoming, and the kinds of relationships that truly support that evolution. This next chapter asks you to balance individuality with interdependence. You don’t have to lose yourself in order to love deeply, and you don’t have to do everything alone in order to remain free.

Pisces Sun & Rising:

Pisces, July’s the month where your biggest growth comes from realizing you don’t have to choose between building a meaningful life and building a meaningful career. Your ruler Jupiter is spending its first full month in Leo, activating your wellness, work, and routines sector for the first time in over a decade. This is a beautiful transit for finding more joy in your daily life, improving your health, growing your business, attracting better work opportunities, or simply creating routines that support the future you’re trying to build. At the same time, Cancer Season and Mercury retrograde activate your creativity, romance, and inner child sector, reminding you not to become so focused on productivity that you forget why you’re working so hard in the first place. Revisit the creative project, reconnect with the hobby you abandoned, or reach back out to someone who reminds you how to laugh. Joy fuels your purpose this month.

Meanwhile, Neptune, your ruling planet, stations retrograde on July 7th, followed by Saturn retrograde on the 26th, both activating your money and self-worth sector. This is a powerful invitation to examine your relationship with abundance. Are you still underestimating your value? Are you pricing yourself according to old versions of yourself instead of the person you’ve become? Over the next few months, you’ll have opportunities to rebuild your confidence from the inside out and create a healthier relationship with earning, receiving, and believing you deserve more. The greatest financial shift this month will happen in the story you tell yourself about what you’re worthy of.

The final week of July opens an entirely new eighteen-month chapter. Mercury stations direct, Leo Season gains momentum, and the lunar nodes shift into Aquarius and Leo, activating your healing and wellness axis. The Aquarius Full Moon on July 29th may bring a profound realization about what you’re finally ready to release such as a habit, fear, or a version of yourself that kept choosing survival over peace. As the North Node leaves your sign and begins its journey through Aquarius, you’re being reminded that healing is a way of living that creates more room for your intuition, your gifts, and the life you’re here to experience.

title: Jupiter In Leo Is Here — But Don’t Fly Too Close To The Sun
text:
Jupiter In Leo Is Here — But Don’t Fly Too Close To The Sun

Now that the prosperous planet Jupiter is entering Leo on June 30th, the theatrics will be immense until the cycle ends on July 26th, 2027 — so we have plenty of time to play the game. 

Jupiter has a near-12-year orbit, landing on the former dates: August 18th, 1990, to September 12th, 1991; August 1st, 2002, to August 27th, 2003; July 16th, 2014, to August 11th, 2015; and so on. Looking back at the plots from these timelines can be beneficial in sussing out and understanding what is coming. Granted, every transit is peppered with different vibes, but the overall theme remains the same. 

Jupiter in Leo motivates us to take a leap of faith and believe in our visions. We are our best cheerleaders, motivating ourselves to be the top cat, creatives, and have an enthusiastic approach to life. Our emotions are big and filled with generosity. The caveat is that Jupiter’s shadow in Leo can make us egotistical and arrogant. We’ll be feeling ourselves, but at a massive level. As a result, our hubris makes us feel we can achieve and conquer anything and everything — but don’t fly too close to the sun. 

Several dates vastly impact this celestial trance. When Jupiter in Leo opposes Pluto retrograde in Aquarius on July 20th, power dynamics surface and pop. We may try to dominate others, or vice versa, creating extreme frustrations. Also, we are going to triangulate relationships by involving people to avoid direct conflict. Ultimately, this is an unhealthy way to deal with others because it shows that the partnership or friendship is lacking. Rather than confront the issues, the unhappy person chooses to cope through gossip, manipulation, avoidance, and emotional or physical affairs. 

Jupiter and Neptune in Aries harmonize on July 20th, heightening our expectations for love and magnifying our passions. Fairy tales can come true if we believe in the story. It’ll be four more years until they train each other again. Jupiter in Leo and Uranus in Gemini come into contact on July 21st, uniting the desire for expansion with societal changes. This is a moment when we can shift the paradigm. The next time these two are in sextile to each other will be in June 2035. Fortunately, the sun and Jupiter’s interwoven influence on July 29th gets us out of these sticky situations and glows up our auras. 

Jupiter in Leo and Saturn in Aries come together on August 31st, April 3, 2027, and July 11th, 2027, forcing us to find a middle ground. Instead of being too austere or happy-go-lucky, we’ll marry Saturn’s need for structure with Jupiter’s joie de vivre mentality. Blending the two juxtaposed elements creates the perfect balance between the two intense sentiments. 

Jupiter in Leo links up with the South Node of Destiny on November 12th, February 22nd, 2027, and April 23rd, 2027. Contrary to what many might think, this is a time of luck. The South Node encourages us to revisit the past, while Jupiter offers us wisdom about our behavior or situations. The alliance urges us to learn from our mistakes and watch for red flags when they recur. If we listen to our intuition and allow old habits to die hard, we can thrive without the ramifications of former matters. The universe has our back and won’t let us fall. 

Mars and Jupiter form a conjunction in Leo on November 16th, close to the South Node, pushing us to take charge. Even if we falter, we will still try our best to succeed. Watch out for arguments with those who put a damper on our plans; the problems can intensify quickly. It would be remiss not to mention Mars’s backward spin in 2027 because the planetary moonwalk gets close to Jupiter retrograde, when it turns direct on April 1st, 2027. This aspect requires us to seek validation from ourselves and not from others. Both are moving along the South Node in March 2027, making us feel temperamental and low-energy. 

Jupiter retrograde in Leo, which lasts from December 12th to April 12th, 2027, offers a moment of reflection. Think about how you want to do good for earth and those who inhabit the planet. Remember, Jupiter in Leo wants to be a superstar; however, the lion roars in a friendly, gentle way to assist others. The lesson is to lead with the heart and love.

title: Your Horoscope This Week: June 28 To July 4
text:
Your Horoscope This Week: June 28 To July 4

Cosmic beings, this week feels like standing at the intersection of every element. Mars enters Gemini on June 28th after six steady weeks in Taurus, bringing movement, curiosity, and momentum back into the collective. If Mars in Taurus taught us that meaningful progress happens one intentional step at a time, Mars in Gemini reminds us that sometimes momentum comes from simply saying yes and figuring it out along the way. Interestingly, this shift happens just one day before Mercury, Gemini’s ruling planet, stations retrograde. Rather than canceling each other out, these transits work together beautifully. Mars says, “Keep moving.” Mercury says, “Just make sure you’re moving toward something that still matters.” Think back to the goals you set at the beginning of 2026. This is an excellent six-week period to finish what you started, especially if you focus on one personal goal and one professional goal instead of scattering your energy across ten different directions.

Then, on June 29th, Mercury begins its three-week retrograde through Cancer, inviting us to revisit our emotional world with greater honesty. Conversations from the past may return. Family dynamics may ask for another look, or old memories may surface — not to trap us there, but to show us how much we’ve grown. The biggest lesson of this retrograde is not to believe every emotion in the exact moment you feel it. Cancer energy is beautifully intuitive, but when emotions become overwhelming, intuition can quickly turn into projection. If you find yourself overthinking, becoming passive-aggressive, or taking things more personally than usual, let your body help your mind catch up. Go for a walk, stretch, journal, swim, or meditate.

That same day, the Capricorn Full Moon offers a grounding counterbalance. While Mars energizes the Air element and Mercury retrograde deepens the Water element, this Full Moon reminds us to stay rooted in reality. Something you’ve been building over the past six months is reaching a meaningful milestone, especially if you’ve remained patient and consistent. Celebrate your progress before immediately chasing the next goal. Sometimes acknowledging how far you’ve come is exactly what gives you the confidence to keep going.

The week closes with one of the biggest astrological milestones of the year: Jupiter enters Leo on June 30th for the first time in over a decade. Jupiter’s year in Cancer expanded our emotional intelligence, intuition, and capacity to nurture ourselves and the people we love. Now Jupiter in Leo asks us to express those lessons with courage. Over the next thirteen months, we’ll all be encouraged to lead more boldly, create more fearlessly, and stop hiding the gifts we’ve spent years developing. Mercury retrograde means July begins as more of a warm-up than a sprint, but that’s a gift in disguise. Stretch the muscles. Refine the vision. By the time Mercury stations direct later in July, you’ll be ready to step into this new era with both confidence and clarity.

Read your horoscopes for your Sun and Rising signs for the most in-depth forecast.

DashDividers_1_500x100

Aries Sun & Rising:

Aries, June refuses to let you coast into July. As your ruling planet, Mars, enters Gemini this week, your mind speeds up and your creativity comes back online. If you’ve been sitting on a writing project, creative idea, newsletter, or conversation you’ve been meaning to have, the next six weeks are your green light. At the same time, Mercury stations retrograde in Cancer, bringing your attention back to home, family, and your emotional foundation. Old family dynamics or unfinished conversations may need your attention, but don’t let temporary distractions convince you you’ve lost your momentum. You’re building something bigger than this moment, and it’s worth protecting your focus while still tending to what matters at home.

The week ends with two major milestones. On the 29th, the Capricorn Full Moon illuminates your career sector, bringing a culmination, promotion, launch, celebration, or realization about the direction you’re heading professionally. Give yourself permission to acknowledge how far you’ve come before immediately chasing the next goal. Then, on June 30th, Jupiter enters Leo for the first time in over a decade, activating your sector of creativity, visibility, romance, and joyful self-expression for the next thirteen months. If the Full Moon is the graduation ceremony, Jupiter in Leo is the acceptance letter to your next adventure. The spotlight finds you naturally now. Your only job is to keep showing up as the version of yourself you’ve spent the past few years becoming.

Taurus Sun & Rising:

Taurus, the week begins with Mars leaving your sign after six weeks of reminding you what it feels like to truly show up for yourself. Chances are you’ve learned a lot about your body, your confidence, your boundaries, and the kind of life you actually want to build. Now Mars enters Gemini, activating your money and self-worth sector for the next six weeks. You’re being encouraged to think beyond your usual approach to making money and recognize that abundance doesn’t have to come from just one predictable path. Meanwhile, Mercury stations retrograde in Cancer, slowing down your communication sector. Conversations from the past may resurface, old friends might pop back into your inbox, or you may simply catch yourself replaying interactions more than usual. Use this retrograde as a chance to reflect without getting so caught up in your thoughts that you stop taking action altogether.

The Capricorn Full Moon on the 29th illuminates your expansion sector, making the end of June feel like a well-earned milestone. A course of study, travel plans, entrepreneurial leap, or long-term vision you’ve been working toward over the past six months may finally begin coming together. Give yourself credit for every uncomfortable step that brought you here. Then, on June 30th, Jupiter enters Leo, beginning a thirteen-month chapter centered around home, family, roots, and emotional security. There’s beautiful potential to build a life that feels deeply nourishing, but it’ll ask you to loosen your grip on needing things to unfold exactly your way. The more you’re willing to create peace instead of power struggles, the more this Jupiter transit can become one of the most rewarding foundations you’ve built in years.

Gemini Sun & Rising:

Gemini, your season may be over, but the momentum is just getting started. Mars enters your sign this week, kicking off a six-week period of increased confidence, courage, and initiative. If you’ve been waiting for the “right time” to pitch the idea, start the project, send the email, or simply put yourself out there, this transit gives you the nudge you’ve been looking for. Just one day later, though, Mercury, your ruling planet, stations retrograde in Cancer and your money sector. Before saying yes to every opportunity, take time to review your budget, pricing, contracts, and relationship with self-worth. Mars wants you to move; Mercury wants you to move with intention. Those two energies can work beautifully together if you let reflection sharpen your next decision rather than stop it altogether.

The week ends on an exciting note. The Capricorn Full Moon illuminates your sector of transformation, shared resources, and long-term investments on June 29th, bringing a six-month chapter to a meaningful turning point. A financial milestone, debt payoff, business partnership, or emotional breakthrough may remind you just how much you’ve grown since the start of the year. Then, on June 30th, Jupiter enters Leo, activating your communication, creativity, and learning sector for the next thirteen months. Your words carry more weight now. Whether you’re writing, teaching, creating content, speaking, or building a brand, this is one of the luckiest periods in over a decade to let your voice reach farther than ever before.

Cancer Sun & Rising:

Cancer, you’ve officially entered one of the most meaningful stretches of the year. Mars has left your friendship sector and entered Gemini, slowing the pace externally while increasing the activity behind the scenes. Over the next six weeks, you’ll accomplish more by protecting your energy than by constantly being available. At the same time, Mercury stations retrograde in your sign on the 29th, making this a deeply personal review of your identity, your priorities, and the story you’ve been telling yourself about who you’re becoming. If you’ve been running on autopilot, this retrograde gives you permission to pause long enough to hear your own intuition again. Some of your biggest breakthroughs this month won’t happen in public… they’ll happen in the quiet moments when you finally admit what you really want.

The Capricorn Full Moon shines a light on your relationship sector, bringing clarity, commitment, or closure to an important partnership that’s been evolving over the past six months. Whether it’s romantic, professional, or deeply personal, the dynamic is asking for honesty and emotional maturity. Then, on June 30th, Jupiter begins its thirteen-month journey through Leo, activating your money and self-worth sector. This is one of the luckiest financial cycles you’ve experienced in over a decade, but it also asks you to stop undervaluing your gifts. Charge what your work is worth, invest in the skills that expand your confidence, and remember that abundance tends to grow wherever you consistently choose yourself.

Leo Sun & Rising:

Leo, the energy changes fast this week, and you’re one of the zodiac signs who will feel it most. Mars enters Gemini, activating your friendship, community, and long-term vision sector for the next six weeks. Suddenly, the right introductions, collaborations, and conversations have the power to open doors you couldn’t have forced open on your own. At the same time, Mercury stations retrograde in Cancer, encouraging you to be more selective with your energy. You don’t need to attend every event, answer every text immediately, or constantly be “on.” Some of your best ideas will arrive when you’re unplugged, well-rested, and giving yourself enough space to hear your own thoughts. Protect your peace without disappearing from opportunities that genuinely excite you.

Then on the 29th, the Capricorn Full Moon brings a meaningful milestone in your work, routines, or health. You may complete a project, establish a healthier rhythm, or finally recognize that the little habits you’ve been building over the past six months are paying off. Then comes the headline of the week: on June 30th, Jupiter enters your sign for the first time in over a decade! This thirteen-month chapter is about expansion, visibility, confidence, and allowing yourself to take up space without apologizing for it. Life’s about to ask more of you because you’re capable of more than you were twelve years ago. Let yourself be seen, but remember that the brightest glow comes from staying connected to who you are, not from constantly chasing applause.

Virgo Sun & Rising:

Virgo, this week’s astrology asks you to balance ambition with perspective. Mars enters Gemini, energizing your career and legacy sector for the next six weeks, making this an excellent time to pitch yourself, apply for opportunities, take on more leadership, or finally move forward with a professional goal that’s been sitting on your to-do list. Just remember that Mercury, your ruling planet, stations retrograde in Cancer one day later, activating your friendship and community sector. A former collaborator could circle back, an old opportunity may resurface, or you might realize that the people around you have a bigger impact on your motivation than you initially thought. Reconnect with the relationships that genuinely inspire you instead of feeling obligated to maintain every connection equally.

Then on the 29th the Capricorn Full Moon illuminates your sector of creativity, romance, and inner child healing, bringing a six-month chapter to a beautiful turning point. A creative project may be ready to launch, a relationship could deepen, or you may simply remember what it feels like to create because it brings you joy, not because it’s productive. Then, on June 30th, Jupiter enters Leo, beginning a thirteen-month journey through your spirituality and healing sector. While the world may seem louder around you, your greatest luck will come from protecting moments of solitude, trusting your intuition, and giving yourself permission to grow behind the scenes before making your next big reveal.

Libra Sun & Rising:

Libra, this week reminds you that growth doesn’t always happen close to home. Mars enters Gemini, activating your expansion, travel, publishing, and higher learning sector for the next six weeks, making you eager to say yes to experiences that stretch your perspective. Whether that’s booking a trip, applying for a program, launching something internationally, or finally sharing an idea you’ve been sitting on, momentum is building. At the same time, Mercury stations retrograde in Cancer, asking you to revisit your career goals and long-term vision. A previous professional opportunity may resurface, or you might realize that the definition of success you had six months ago no longer fits who you’re becoming. Give yourself permission to edit the plan instead of clinging to it.

The Capricorn Full Moon illuminates your home and family sector on June 29th, bringing a meaningful culmination around your living situation, emotional foundation, or family dynamics. Something you’ve been working through behind the scenes over the past six months is ready to reach a turning point, giving you a stronger sense of stability as you move forward. Then, on June 30th, Jupiter enters Leo, beginning a thirteen-month chapter of growth through friendships, community, collaboration, and long-term dreams. The people entering your life now aren’t here by accident. Stay open to unexpected introductions, join rooms that challenge you to think bigger, and remember that your next breakthrough is just as likely to come through your network as through your own efforts.

Scorpio Sun & Rising:

Scorpio, you’re entering one of those weeks where the biggest breakthroughs happen beneath the surface first. Mars enters Gemini, activating your sector of transformation, intimacy, and long-term wealth for the next six weeks. This is a powerful time to renegotiate a contract, pay down debt, invest more intentionally, or have the honest conversations you’ve been avoiding around money or emotional vulnerability. Then Mercury stations retrograde in fellow Water sign Cancer, asking you to revisit your beliefs about what’s possible. You may find yourself returning to an old travel dream, educational goal, business vision, or spiritual practice that still feels deeply aligned. Don’t dismiss it just because it isn’t brand new. Sometimes Mercury retrograde reminds you that your future has been quietly calling your name for a while.

The Capricorn Full Moon lights up your communication sector on the 29th, bringing clarity to a conversation, writing project, teaching opportunity, or decision that’s been unfolding over the past six months. You’ll likely notice that you’re expressing yourself with far more confidence than you were at the beginning of the year. Then, on June 30th, Jupiter enters Leo, launching a thirteen-month cycle of career growth, visibility, and leadership. This is one of the biggest professional transits you’ve experienced in over a decade. Recognition tends to find the people who have consistently shown up for their craft, and you’ve been laying that foundation for a long time. Don’t shrink yourself when bigger opportunities arrive. Your next level will ask you to lead with both confidence and integrity.

Sagittarius Sun & Rising:

Sag, your ruling planet is about to begin one of its biggest chapters in over a decade, and you can already feel the buildup. But first, Mars enters Gemini, activating your relationship sector for the next six weeks and bringing more momentum to your closest one-on-one connections. New collaborators, romantic interests, or business partners may enter your orbit, while existing relationships ask for more honesty and initiative. Then Mercury stations retrograde in Cancer, shifting your attention toward trust, intimacy, and shared resources. Financial agreements, investments, inheritances, or conversations about emotional vulnerability may need another look. Don’t rush to solve everything immediately. Sometimes the strongest relationships are built by giving important conversations enough time to unfold.

On the 29th the Capricorn Full Moon illuminates your money and self-worth sector, marking a rewarding milestone around something you’ve been building over the past six months. You may receive recognition, hit a financial goal, or simply realize you’ve become far more confident about what your time, talent, and energy are worth. Then, on June 30th, your ruling planet Jupiter enters fellow Fire sign Leo, kicking off a thirteen-month cycle of expansion through travel, entrepreneurship, publishing, higher education, and bold new adventures. If life has felt like it’s been asking you to play small lately, that chapter’s ending. The next year is about saying yes to experiences that make your world bigger while trusting that you’re more prepared than you think.

Capricorn Sun & Rising:

Capricorn, there’s something refreshing about this week’s energy because it reminds you that you don’t have to carry everything by yourself. Mars enters Gemini, energizing your work, wellness, and daily routine sector for the next six weeks, making this an excellent time to streamline your schedule, delegate where you can, and build systems that actually support the life you’re trying to create. Then Mercury stations retrograde in Cancer, bringing your relationships into focus. A conversation with a partner, client, close friend, or even an ex could resurface, giving you the opportunity to approach the situation with greater maturity than you would have six months ago. Listen carefully before jumping into problem-solving mode. Sometimes people simply want to feel understood before they feel advised.

The Capricorn Full Moon lands in your sign, making you one of the main characters of the week. A personal goal, identity shift, or milestone you’ve been working toward since the beginning of the year reaches a meaningful culmination, and you’re finally able to appreciate how much you’ve grown. Then, on June 30th, Jupiter enters Leo, beginning a thirteen-month cycle of transformation, intimacy, shared wealth, and emotional depth. This transit has the potential to bring greater financial support, stronger partnerships, and profound inner healing, but it asks you to let people in a little more than you’re used to. Your next level won’t be built entirely on self-reliance. Some of your greatest blessings over the coming year will arrive through trust, collaboration, and the courage to share the weight.

Aquarius Sun & Rising:

Aquarius, this week’s astrology reminds you that life isn’t only about the big vision — it’s also about how you show up for the people and passions that make the vision worthwhile. Mars enters fellow Air sign Gemini, activating your creativity, romance, and inner child sector for the next six weeks. Inspiration comes quickly now, and you’re likely to feel more playful, flirtatious, and willing to take creative risks than you have in a while. Then Mercury stations retrograde in Cancer, turning your attention back to your routines, work-life balance, and overall well-being. If you’ve been saying yes to everyone except yourself, this retrograde asks you to recalibrate. Revisit the habits that genuinely make you feel your best instead of trying to optimize every minute of the day.

The Capricorn Full Moon strikes on the 29th and illuminates your spirituality and healing sector, bringing a quiet but powerful sense of closure to something you’ve been processing internally over the past six months. You may release an old fear, forgive yourself for a past version of you, or simply notice that what once consumed your thoughts no longer carries the same weight. Then, on June 30th, Jupiter enters Leo, beginning a thirteen-month cycle of growth through partnerships, love, collaboration, and meaningful one-on-one connections. Some of the biggest opportunities of the coming year won’t be things you accomplish alone. They’ll arrive through the people who challenge you, champion you, and remind you that even the most independent soul benefits from having someone in their corner.

Pisces Sun & Rising:

Pisces, this week’s astrology reminds you that your imagination deserves a tangible outlet. Mars enters fellow Mutable sign Gemini, activating your home and family sector for the next six weeks, making it an ideal time to reorganize your space, move, redecorate, reconnect with loved ones, or finally tackle the home project that’s been sitting on your list. Then Mercury stations retrograde in fellow Water sign Cancer, lighting up your creativity, romance, and inner child sector. An old creative idea may suddenly feel exciting again, someone from your romantic past could reappear, or you may rediscover a hobby that reminds you who you were before life became so serious. Follow what brings you genuine joy without feeling pressured to have every next step mapped out.

The Capricorn Full Moon illuminates your friendship and community sector on the 29th, bringing a meaningful culmination around the people you’ve chosen to surround yourself with over the past six months. You may celebrate a shared accomplishment, naturally outgrow a connection, or realize just how much your support system has evolved. Then, on June 30th, Jupiter enters Leo, beginning a thirteen-month cycle of growth through your work, routines, health, and daily habits. The biggest wins of this transit won’t come from one dramatic breakthrough but from the small promises you keep to yourself every day. As your routines become more aligned with your purpose, you’ll discover that abundance has a way of showing up naturally when your everyday life supports the future you’re working toward.

title: The Strawberry Full Moon In Capricorn Is Here — & You Need To Take A Leap
text:
The Strawberry Full Moon In Capricorn Is Here — & You Need To Take A Leap

The first Full Moon of the summer comes at the end of June, in the sign of Capricorn, heightening our expectations and anticipation for the future. June 29th marks a magical day, so set intentions that speak to your soul and manifest what appeals to your sentiments. The micromoon is the effect of the earth and moon being at apogee, making the moon appear smaller than usual. Don’t let its size fool you — it still packs a load of emotion for us. 

Beware, Neptune in Aries crafts a fraught alliance with the Full Moon, illuminating our ambitions. We must look within ourselves and figure out what makes us happy — it could be a childhood dream we are beginning to realize in the coming weeks. Letting go of the fear we attach to the unknown is hard because it requires us to relinquish control over the direction and outcome. The flip side is that if we don’t take this leap, it will burden us until we do. Tie in the fruitful link to the Nodes of Destiny, which are on the Pisces and Virgo lunar axis, to enhance the fateful call that propels us to innovation. 

The energy echoes the conjunction of Saturn and Neptune in Aries, which began on February 20th. Now that some time has passed, we are discerning how to utilize the transformative astrological aspect that augmented our values and ideals. The lunation asks us to restructure our visions. The Full Moon in Capricorn, which Saturn rules, is shedding light on our plans and desires. The element of how we can pragmatically assert our passions into reality is the clincher; therefore, it’ll require proper preparation and forethought — but it’s doable with a combination of patience and vigor. Never give up on what we hold in our hearts and want to bring to life.

There is an element of healing brought on by Mars in Gemini, sharing a minor frustration with the centaur Chiron in Taurus. Our former partnerships might come back to haunt us, due to Mercury’s cosmic spin, inflaming wounds that need to be mended and resolved. Understanding our core issues will be difficult and tearful; consider channeling the vibe into healthy activities like sports, yoga, or art. Learning not to be aggressive in how we express ourselves will be pivotal, as well as distancing ourselves from the naysayers. We may even realize that holding onto resentments is weighing on our auras and choose to free ourselves from the negativity of those who do not believe in us.

Speaking of such individuals, Mercury has some insight for us on that matter. The same day, Mercury retrograde begins its planetary moonwalk in Cancer (a little over six hours before the Full Moon, to be precise). When Mercury begins to move backward in the sign of Cancer, it will make us all reflective and contemplative of the past. Nostalgia is the key ingredient and theme that urges us to reconnect with a former aspiration and to seek approval from those we care about. We are taking stock of whether or not our peers are on our side. Don’t jump into reuniting these relationships until there is certainty that they’ll work out. If need be, remaining nuzzled in a cocoon for safety and TLC until we are ready to engage with others is the best form of protection before sharing the goals we yearn to create or elevate. 

The caveat is that it’ll take a hot minute for our agendas to move forward. However, we should take baby steps towards cultivating our incentives. Shooting our shot will lead to success, just not on our timeline, which is okay. The bottom line is that we must keep the faith — even if we appear to be delulu. Individualization and acceptance push us to inspire ourselves and the masses. Take flight to a state of enlightenment!

title: Inside Daniella Kallmeyer’s Birthday Party — & The Chic Summer Outfit Ideas Guests Wore
text:
Inside Daniella Kallmeyer’s Birthday Party — & The Chic Summer Outfit Ideas Guests Wore

Last night, Daniella Kallmeyer kicked off the summer party season with an unforgettable birthday soirée. The visually stunning design studio, Apparatus, was transformed into Cafe Kallmeyer for the night with fashion insiders, actresses, and models putting their summer party looks to the test.

Of course, Kallmeyer made jaws drop in a sculptural wrap paired with coordinating trousers. To help her toast the evening, Severance actress Britt Lower, And Just Like That star Sarita Choudhury, and “Alaska” singer Maggie Rogers were among the chic revelers in the building. All in all, the festivities served as a chic lesson in what to wear to a friend’s birthday party. Whether you’re getting together for a rooftop gathering or a dance rager, take notes from last night’s cool attendees.

DashDividers_1_500x100

The Elevated Graphic Tee & Statement Pants

Ok, maybe not all of us have friends who are major fashion designers, but you can still show your support with playful merch that honors the birthday girl. Here, Lower reps the honoree with a Kall Gal tee. The biggest lesson here is all about balance. If you’re going super casual on the top with a tee, elevate the look with a fancy pair of pants like these Fall 2026 Kallmeyer embroidered beauties.

High-Texture Neutrals & Asymmetric Silhouettes

You never want to outshine your friend, but you can still make a subtle statement. The classiest way to do so is with lush texture and asymmetric silhouettes à la Choudhury. Bonus points if your shoes are easy to kick off for when the party really turns up on the dance floor.

Sharp, Deconstructed Tailoring

Exacting tailoring will always be your friend for sharp day-to-night dressing. Follow Louisa Jacobson’s lead and wear a white-on-white tie and button-down shirt. Let timeless trousers add the finishing touches to the polished party look.

Sheer Tops & Subversive Basics

Breezy sheer tops feel special enough for the moment without overdoing it. Maggie Rogers demonstrates how to execute the party-ready look with black separates and a long pearl necklace.

Tactile Knits & Pop-of-Color Utility

Playful pops of color like red and mustard will rise to the occasion. Keep the cool-girl vibes going by pairing tops with utilitarian cargo pants and colorblocking bags.

The “Borrow-from-the-Boys” Cool-Girl Uniform and The Ruched Maxi

A menswear-inspired look always takes the cake, but if you’re in the mood for something on the other side of the sartorial spectrum, turn to a trusty maxi dress to celebrate the evening in.
